description: Identity — Magic link auth service, field encryption, Kong JWT, auth UI, user profiles, onboarding

tasks.title: Field Encryption Library — LocalFile implementation
tasks.prompt:
Implement the LocalFileFieldEncryptor in Snapp.Shared/Encryption/ as specified in SNAPP-TRD.md WU-1.1.

The IFieldEncryptor interface already exists from Sprint 1. Implement:

1. `LocalFileFieldEncryptor.cs`:
   - Reads 256-bit master key from local file path (config: Encryption:LocalKeyPath)
   - EncryptAsync(plaintext):
     a. Generate random 256-bit data key (RandomNumberGenerator)
     b. Encrypt plaintext with AES-256-GCM using data key
     c. Encrypt data key with master key (AES-256-GCM)
     d. Return Base64([2 bytes: encrypted data key length][N bytes: encrypted data key][12 bytes: GCM nonce][ciphertext][16 bytes: GCM auth tag])
   - DecryptAsync(ciphertext): reverse of above
   - EncryptWithKeyIdAsync: returns (encrypted, "local")

2. Update `Hosting/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs` AddSnappEncryption:
   - If config Encryption:Provider == "LocalFile" → register LocalFileFieldEncryptor
   - Read key path from config

3. Create encryption unit tests in test/Snapp.Shared.Tests/Encryption/:
   - Round-trip: encrypt then decrypt returns original
   - Different plaintexts produce different ciphertexts
   - Tampering with ciphertext causes decryption failure (auth tag check)
   - Empty string round-trips
   - Unicode text round-trips
   - Plaintext data key not retained after operation

4. Generate a dev master key if not present: src/Snapp.Infrastructure/Docker/keys/dev-master.key (32 random bytes)

All tests must pass.
tasks.done_when: LocalFileFieldEncryptor compiles and all encryption tests pass. Round-trip works. Tampered ciphertext rejected.

tasks.title: Magic Link Auth Service — Docker container
tasks.prompt:
Implement the complete Magic Link Auth Service as specified in SNAPP-TRD.md WU-1.2.

Create Snapp.Service.Auth with:

1. Program.cs:
   - Minimal API with AddOpenApi(), MapOpenApi()
   - Register DynamoDB client (ServiceURL from config for local, default for AWS)
   - Register IFieldEncryptor (LocalFile for dev)
   - Register IEmailSender (SmtpEmailSender for dev)
   - Dual-host pattern: #if LAMBDA / await app.RunLambdaAsync(); / #else / app.Run();
   - Map all auth endpoints

2. Endpoints/AuthEndpoints.cs — 4 endpoints, each with full OpenAPI metadata:

   POST /api/auth/magic-link
   - Generate 64-char URL-safe code (RandomNumberGenerator)
   - Rate limit: 3 per email per 15 min (DynamoDB conditional write with TTL)
   - Hash email SHA-256 for storage
   - Store TOKEN# item in snapp-users table with 15-min TTL
   - Send email via SMTP with magic link URL
   - Return 200 regardless of whether email exists (security)

   POST /api/auth/validate
   - Look up TOKEN# by code, verify not expired
   - Delete token (single-use)
   - Look up EMAIL# → userId, or create new user if first login
   - New user: create USER#/PROFILE, encrypt email → USER#/PII, create EMAIL# index
   - Generate JWT (RS256, 15 min) with sub=userId claims
   - Generate refresh token (64 chars), store REFRESH# with 30-day TTL
   - Return TokenResponse { AccessToken, RefreshToken, ExpiresIn, IsNewUser }

   POST /api/auth/refresh
   - Hash refresh token, look up REFRESH#
   - Delete old, issue new JWT + new refresh token (rotation)
   - Return TokenResponse

   POST /api/auth/logout
   - Delete REFRESH# item
   - Return 200 (idempotent)

3. Services/SmtpEmailSender.cs:
   - Implements IEmailSender
   - Uses System.Net.Mail.SmtpClient
   - Host/Port from config (SMTP__HOST, SMTP__PORT)
   - Sends HTML email with magic link

4. Repositories/AuthRepository.cs:
   - All DynamoDB operations for auth (tokens, email lookup, rate limiting)
   - Uses IAmazonDynamoDB with snapp-users table

5. Dockerfile (multi-stage: sdk:9.0 → aspnet:9.0, expose 8080)

6. Add to docker-compose.yml:
   ```
   snapp-auth:
     build:
       context: ../../
       dockerfile: src/Snapp.Service.Auth/Dockerfile
     container_name: snapp-auth
     ports: ["8081:8080"]
     depends_on: [dynamodb-local, papercut]
     environment: (all common env vars)
     volumes: ["../Docker/keys:/keys:ro"]
     networks: [snapp-net]
   ```

7. Update Kong config to route /api/auth/* to snapp-auth:8080

8. Build and test: `docker compose build snapp-auth && docker compose up -d snapp-auth`

Verify the service starts, is reachable through Kong, and the magic link email appears in Papercut.
tasks.done_when: Auth service runs in Docker. POST /api/auth/magic-link through Kong sends email to Papercut. Full auth flow works: magic-link → validate → JWT. Refresh and logout work. Rate limiting works.

tasks.title: Activate auth integration tests
tasks.prompt:
The auth integration tests from Sprint 1.5 (test/Snapp.Service.Auth.Tests/AuthFlowIntegrationTests.cs) were written with Skip attributes because the auth service wasn't deployed yet.

Now that the auth service is running in Docker:

1. Remove all [Fact(Skip = ...)] attributes — replace with plain [Fact]
2. Run the tests: `dotnet test test/Snapp.Service.Auth.Tests/`
3. Fix any test failures:
   - If the API response format differs from what the test expects, update the test to match the actual implementation
   - If PapercutClient can't retrieve emails, debug the Papercut API endpoint and fix the client
   - If Kong returns unexpected status codes, verify the Kong config matches what tests expect
4. Add any additional tests that the actual implementation reveals are needed:
   - Test that the JWT contains correct claims (sub, iss, aud, exp)
   - Test that PII (email) is actually encrypted in DynamoDB — read the raw item and assert the email field is not plaintext
   - Test that the magic link email body contains a valid URL with the code
5. Ensure ALL tests pass with Docker Compose running
6. Run `scripts/start-and-test.sh` to verify the full test pipeline works end-to-end

Do not modify the auth service code — only fix tests. If the service has a genuine bug that prevents tests from passing, document it in BLOCKERS.md.
tasks.done_when: All auth integration tests pass (no Skip attributes remain). PII encryption verified. Full start-and-test.sh pipeline green.

tasks.title: Kong JWT plugin configuration for auth service
tasks.prompt:
Configure Kong to validate JWTs issued by the auth service so that protected routes require authentication.

1. Update Kong declarative config (src/Snapp.Infrastructure/Kong/kong.yml):
   - Add a JWT consumer for the auth service
   - Configure the JWT plugin with the RS256 public key from src/Snapp.Infrastructure/Docker/keys/
   - Apply JWT plugin to ALL routes EXCEPT:
     - POST /api/auth/magic-link
     - POST /api/auth/validate
     - POST /api/auth/refresh
     - Any /health endpoints
   - Protected routes: /api/users/*, /api/networks/*, /api/content/*, /api/intel/*, /api/tx/*, /api/notif/*, /api/linkedin/*, POST /api/auth/logout

2. Configure the JWT signing key:
   - The auth service signs JWTs with the RSA private key at /keys/jwt-signing.pem
   - Kong needs the corresponding public key to verify
   - Extract public key: `openssl rsa -in jwt-signing.pem -pubout -out jwt-signing.pub`
   - Configure Kong's JWT plugin with this public key
   - Match the key identifier (iss claim or kid header) between auth service and Kong

3. Verify by testing:
   - `curl http://localhost:8000/api/users/me` → 401 (no token)
   - Complete auth flow → get JWT
   - `curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer {jwt}' http://localhost:8000/api/users/me` → NOT 401 (may be 503 if user service not running, but should pass auth)
   - `curl http://localhost:8000/api/auth/magic-link -X POST -d '{"email":"test@test.com"}'` → should work WITHOUT a token

4. Update scripts/test-kong-routes.sh to verify JWT plugin is active on protected routes

5. Restart Kong after config changes: `docker compose restart kong`
tasks.done_when: Kong validates JWTs on protected routes. Unprotected auth endpoints work without tokens. Protected endpoints return 401 without valid JWT. test-kong-routes.sh validates this.

tasks.title: Auth UI — Login and callback pages
tasks.prompt:
Implement the login and magic link callback pages in Snapp.Client using MudBlazor, as specified in SNAPP-TRD.md WU-1.5 and the UX spec at docs/ux/login-spec.md.

1. Pages/Auth/Login.razor:
   - Clean centered MudCard layout
   - MudTextField for email with validation ([EmailAddress])
   - MudButton "Send Login Link" (MudButton Variant=Filled Color=Primary)
   - States:
     a. Default: email input + button
     b. Sending: MudButton disabled with MudProgressCircular
     c. Sent: MudAlert Severity=Success "Check your email for a login link" with MudText "Didn't receive it?" + MudButton Variant=Text "Send again"
     d. Error: MudAlert Severity=Error with message + retry
   - Calls auth API: POST /api/auth/magic-link via HttpClient

2. Pages/Auth/Callback.razor:
   - Route: /login/callback with [Parameter] [SupplyParameterFromQuery] string Code
   - On init: call POST /api/auth/validate with code
   - States:
     a. Validating: MudProgressCircular with MudText "Signing you in..."
     b. Success: store tokens in AuthState, redirect:
        - If IsNewUser → NavigateTo("/onboarding")
        - If existing → NavigateTo("/")
     c. Failed: MudAlert Severity=Error "This link has expired or already been used" + MudButton → /login

3. Update SnappAuthStateProvider:
   - On successful validate: store AccessToken + RefreshToken in localStorage
   - NotifyAuthenticationStateChanged()
   - GetAuthenticationStateAsync: parse JWT claims into ClaimsPrincipal

4. Update AuthDelegatingHandler:
   - Attach Bearer token to all /api/* requests (except /api/auth/magic-link, /api/auth/validate, /api/auth/refresh)
   - On 401: attempt refresh, retry once, if still 401 → clear auth, navigate to /login

5. Update MainLayout.razor:
   - Show MudAppBar profile menu when authenticated (MudMenu with avatar placeholder + "Logout" MudMenuItem)
   - Logout: calls /api/auth/logout, clears auth state, navigates to /login

6. Write bUnit tests:
   - Login page renders email field and button
   - Submitting valid email shows "sent" state
   - Callback with valid code redirects
   - Callback with invalid code shows error
   - Logout clears state

Test manually: run Blazor app, navigate to /login, enter email, check Papercut for the email, click the link, verify redirect.
tasks.done_when: Login page renders with MudBlazor. Email submission calls API. Callback validates code and stores JWT. Auth state persists. Logout works. bUnit tests pass.

tasks.title: User Profile Service — Docker container
tasks.prompt:
Implement the User Profile Service as specified in SNAPP-TRD.md WU-1.6.

Create Snapp.Service.User with:

1. Program.cs: same pattern as auth service (Minimal API, DI, dual-host, OpenAPI)

2. Endpoints/UserEndpoints.cs — all with OpenAPI metadata:

   GET /api/users/me
   - userId from JWT claims (sub)
   - Get USER#/PROFILE from snapp-users
   - Return ProfileResponse

   GET /api/users/{userId}
   - Return public fields only (no PII)

   PUT /api/users/me
   - Update non-null fields from UpdateProfileRequest
   - Recalculate ProfileCompleteness (DisplayName +20, Specialty +20, Geography +20, LinkedIn +15, PracticeData +15, Photo +10)
   - Return updated ProfileResponse

   POST /api/users/me/onboard
   - Accept OnboardingRequest
   - Update PROFILE with DisplayName, Specialty, Geography
   - If email/phone provided: encrypt via IFieldEncryptor, store in PII item
   - Recalculate completeness
   - Return ProfileResponse

   GET /api/users/me/pii
   - Self only — decrypt and return email, phone

   GET /api/users/search?specialty={s}&geo={g}
   - Query GSI-Specialty
   - Return list of ProfileResponse (public fields)

3. Repositories/UserRepository.cs implementing IUserRepository

4. Dockerfile + add to docker-compose.yml as snapp-user (port 8082)

5. Update Kong config: route /api/users/* → snapp-user:8080 (JWT required)

6. Integration tests in test/Snapp.Service.User.Tests/:
   - GET /me returns profile for authenticated user
   - PUT /me updates fields, recalculates completeness
   - Onboarding stores encrypted PII
   - Raw DynamoDB read confirms PII is NOT plaintext
   - GET /me/pii decrypts correctly
   - GET /{otherId} returns public fields only
   - Unauthenticated → 401

Tests must use DockerTestFixture and authenticate via the real auth flow (magic link through Papercut → get JWT → use JWT for user API calls).
tasks.done_when: User service runs in Docker. Profile CRUD works through Kong with JWT auth. PII encrypted in DynamoDB. Integration tests pass.

tasks.title: Profile and Onboarding UI
tasks.prompt:
Implement profile pages and onboarding wizard using MudBlazor, as specified in SNAPP-TRD.md WU-1.7 and docs/ux/onboarding-spec.md.

1. Pages/Profile/Onboarding.razor:
   - Route: /onboarding (shown after first login)
   - MudStepper with 3 steps:
     Step 1 "About You":
       - MudTextField DisplayName [Required]
       - MudSelect Specialty (dropdown from predefined list in Snapp.Shared constants)
       - MudAutocomplete Geography (state/region)
     Step 2 "Your Practice" (optional, MudButton "Skip" visible):
       - MudTextField Practice name
       - MudSelect Practice size (Solo / 2-5 / 6-10 / 10+)
       - MudNumericField Years in practice
     Step 3 "Connect" (optional, skip-able):
       - MudButton "Link your LinkedIn" (placeholder — wired in S13)
       - MudTextField Phone (optional)
   - MudProgressLinear showing step progress
   - On complete: POST /api/users/me/onboard → NavigateTo("/")

2. Pages/Profile/MyProfile.razor:
   - Route: /profile
   - MudCard with: MudAvatar (placeholder), MudText DisplayName, MudChip Specialty, MudText Geography
   - ProfileCompleteness component (MudProgressLinear)
   - MudButton "Edit Profile" → opens edit mode or navigates to /profile/edit
   - Completeness prompt: MudAlert Severity=Info "Your profile is {X}% complete. {suggestion}"

3. Pages/Profile/ViewProfile.razor:
   - Route: /profile/{userId}
   - Public fields only (no PII)
   - MudCard layout similar to MyProfile but read-only

4. Pages/Profile/EditProfile.razor:
   - MudForm with MudTextField/MudSelect for editable fields
   - MudButton Save → PUT /api/users/me
   - MudButton Cancel → back to view

5. Components/Cards/ProfileCard.razor:
   - Compact MudCard: MudAvatar, name, specialty, geography
   - Reusable in member lists, search results

6. Components/ProfileCompleteness.razor:
   - MudProgressLinear with color based on score (Error <40, Warning <70, Success >=70)
   - MudTooltip with suggestion text

7. bUnit tests for all components

Test manually: login → onboarding wizard → fill out → save → view profile → edit → save.
tasks.done_when: Onboarding wizard works end-to-end with MudStepper. Profile view/edit functional. ProfileCard reusable. Completeness bar renders correctly. bUnit tests pass.

tasks.title: Sprint 2 integration validation
tasks.prompt:
Run the complete test suite and validate the Sprint 2 deliverables end-to-end.

1. Run `scripts/start-and-test.sh` — everything must pass

2. Run all integration tests:
   - `dotnet test test/Snapp.Service.Auth.Tests/` — all auth flow tests
   - `dotnet test test/Snapp.Service.User.Tests/` — all profile tests
   - `dotnet test test/Snapp.Shared.Tests/` — shared library tests
   - `dotnet test test/Snapp.Client.Tests/` — Blazor component tests

3. Perform a manual end-to-end walkthrough and document results:
   a. Open http://localhost:5000 → should see login page
   b. Enter email, submit → should see 'check your email'
   c. Open http://localhost:8025 → should see magic link email
   d. Click magic link → should redirect to /onboarding (new user)
   e. Complete onboarding → should see profile page
   f. Edit profile → should save and reflect changes
   g. Logout → should return to login page
   h. Login again with same email → should go to / (not /onboarding)

4. Verify security:
   - Read raw DynamoDB items for the test user: PII fields are encrypted (not plaintext)
   - Call GET /api/users/me without JWT → 401
   - Call GET /api/users/{id} for another user → only public fields returned

5. If any tests fail, fix them (test code only — if service has bugs, document in BLOCKERS.md)

6. Write a Sprint 2 validation report in .claude/reports/sprint-2-validation.md with:
   - Test results summary (total/pass/fail/skip)
   - Manual walkthrough results
   - Security verification results
   - Any issues found and their status

7. Commit all test fixes and the validation report.
tasks.done_when: All tests pass. Manual walkthrough documented. Security verified. Sprint 2 validation report written. No blockers.
